 {"id":"bd-1s7t","title":"E2E Tests: Logs page filtering and search","description":"End-to-end tests for the Logs page filtering and search functionality.

## Purpose
Test the log viewer with its filtering, level selection,
and search capabilities.

## Test Requirements

### Log Display
- [ ] Logs render with timestamps
- [ ] Log levels color-coded
- [ ] Long logs truncate/wrap correctly
- [ ] Syntax highlighting in log messages

### Level Filtering
- [ ] Filter by ERROR only
- [ ] Filter by WARN and above
- [ ] Filter by INFO and above
- [ ] Filter by DEBUG and above
- [ ] Filter by TRACE (all)

### Text Search
- [ ] Search filter input works
- [ ] Search highlights matches
- [ ] Search is case-insensitive
- [ ] Clear search shows all

### Navigation
- [ ] Scroll through log list
- [ ] Jump to top/bottom
- [ ] Page up/down
- [ ] Line numbers optional

### Real-time Updates
- [ ] New logs appear at bottom
- [ ] Auto-scroll when at bottom
- [ ] Stay position when scrolled up

## Logging Requirements
- [ ] Log filter changes
- [ ] Log search queries
- [ ] Log scroll position changes

## Success Criteria
- Filtering works correctly
- Search finds all matches
- Large log volumes perform well","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DustyMeadow","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:21.701891676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:54:29.341649848Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:54:29.341520717Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","e2e","logs","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-1cgb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-28kk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1s7t","depends_on_id":"bd-t1aq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tdh","title":"Jobs: paginator integration for long lists","description":"Add pagination for long Jobs lists using `bubbles::paginator`.

Why:
- Long lists are a common real-world case; pagination prevents the UI from becoming sluggish.
- It also showcases another Bubbles component in a realistic workflow.

Behavior:
- Paginator reflects total pages and current page.
- Works correctly with filtering/sorting:
  - applying a filter updates total pages
  - selection remains stable (best-effort) when the filtered set changes
- Keyboard:
  - next/prev page shortcuts
  - jump to first/last (optional)

Acceptance:
- Large job sets remain responsive.
- Pagination UX feels consistent with the rest of the app (styled, discoverable).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.084401297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:25:38.457559552Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:25:38.457491325Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","paginator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1u1s","title":"Unit Tests: bubbletea core (Cmd, Message, Model)","description":"Add comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core functionality.

## Current State
bubbletea has basic tests but gaps in:
- Cmd::batch and Cmd::sequence edge cases
- Message type coercion and downcasting
- Model trait default implementations

## Test Requirements

### Cmd Module
- [ ] Cmd::new() with various closure types
- [ ] Cmd::batch() with empty, single, multiple commands
- [ ] Cmd::sequence() ordering guarantees
- [ ] Cmd::blocking() thread behavior
- [ ] Cmd cancellation (if supported)

### Message Module
- [ ] Message::new() with all message types
- [ ] Message::is::<T>() type checking
- [ ] Message::downcast::<T>() success/failure
- [ ] Message cloning and equality

### Key/Mouse Events
- [ ] KeyEvent parsing from raw bytes
- [ ] MouseEvent coordinate handling
- [ ] Modifier key combinations
- [ ] Special keys (F1-F12, arrows, etc.)

## Success Criteria
- 100% coverage of public Cmd API
- 100% coverage of Message API
- All edge cases documented with tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindyLantern","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:21.686576405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673408772Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:54.673341717Z","close_reason":"Added 24 comprehensive unit tests for bubbletea core: Cmd (batch/sequence edge cases, new_optional, blocking, captured values), Message (downcast_ref, Debug format, all built-in types, equality/clone/copy traits). Tests verify 1-command optimization in batch/sequence.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1u1s","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1wtl","title":"Files: preview pane (viewport)","description":"Add a preview pane for the selected file using `bubbles::viewport`.

Why:
- Makes the Files page feel like a real tool (inspect configs/logs/docs quickly).
- Exercises viewport scrolling + resize behavior.

Preview behavior:
- For plain text:
  - show a bounded preview (first N lines/bytes) with a "truncated" indicator
  - choose a clear wrapping strategy (wrap-by-default is fine; optionally support horizontal scroll)
- Show lightweight metadata (optional but nice):
  - file size, last modified, detected type (text/markdown/binary)
- Resize handling:
  - preview reflows correctly on window resize

Acceptance:
- Large files do not lock up the UI; preview work is bounded.
- Scrolling the preview is smooth and does not interfere with file picker navigation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:19.317694097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579944103Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:11:21.579876477Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","viewport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1wtl","depends_on_id":"bd-fdf3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1x3q","title":"Jobs: start/cancel/retry actions via Cmd/AsyncCmd","description":"Make jobs interactive.

Implement actions:
- Start a new job (creates a job + progress updates)
- Cancel job
- Retry failed job

Implementation notes:
- Use the shared domain action API (`demo_showcase data: action API …`) so UI code stays thin.
- bubbletea `Cmd` for sync simulation.
- bubbletea `AsyncCmd` when async feature enabled.

Acceptance:
- Actions produce toasts and update other pages (dashboard/logs).
- Action layer is unit tested independently of the UI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:10:20.940826293Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:24:13.108786778Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:24:13.108709945Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","jobs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1x3q","depends_on_id":"bd-y3c8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xvj","title":"demo_showcase core: Notes scratchpad modal (bubbles::TextArea)","description":"Exercise `bubbles::TextArea` in a way that feels like a real product feature.

Feature idea:
- A modal "Notes" scratchpad to write an incident note / deploy note.
- Supports multi-line editing, copy, clear, save.
- Saving the note emits a structured log entry and a toast.

Why this belongs in core:
- Provides a compelling demo of text editing beyond single-line inputs.
- Gives users a practical pattern they can copy into real apps.

Acceptance:
- Accessible via a global key (defined in UX keymap).
- Uses `bubbles::TextArea` (not huh) so we prove both stacks.
- Saving integrates with the log stream + notifications.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:29.295951524Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:00:55.539426153Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:00:55.539354009Z","close_reason":"Implemented Notes scratchpad modal with full integration: NotesModal component with TextArea, Ctrl+S save, Ctrl+C copy, Ctrl+X clear, Esc close; integrated into app.rs with N key handler, message handling, and overlay rendering; added help keybinding documentation","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase","textarea","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-1qwj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-3pm6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1xvj","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:57:02Z","created_by":"import"}]}

+func captureTextAreaTests(fs *capture.FixtureSet) {
+	// Keep dimensions wide/tall enough to avoid soft-wrap affecting cursor math.
+	const width = 40
+	const height = 6
+
+	// Test 1: Basic textarea creation
+	{
+		ta := textarea.New()
+		ta.SetWidth(width)
+		ta.SetHeight(height)
+		ta.Blur() // Ensure no cursor rendering variability in View()
+
+		fs.AddTestWithCategory("textarea_new", "unit",
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"width":  width,
+				"height": height,
+			},
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"value":       ta.Value(),
+				"focused":     ta.Focused(),
+				"width":       ta.Width(),
+				"height":      ta.Height(),
+				"line":        ta.Line(),
+				"line_count":  ta.LineCount(),
+				"length":      ta.Length(),
+				"placeholder": ta.Placeholder,
+			},
+		)
+	}
+
+	// Test 2: SetValue with multiple lines
+	{
+		ta := textarea.New()
+		ta.SetWidth(width)
+		ta.SetHeight(height)
+		ta.SetValue("Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3")
+		ta.Blur()
+
+		fs.AddTestWithCategory("textarea_set_value", "unit",
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"value":  "Line 1\nLine 2\nLine 3",
+				"width":  width,
+				"height": height,
+			},
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"value":      ta.Value(),
+				"line_count": ta.LineCount(),
+				"length":     ta.Length(),
+				"line":       ta.Line(),
+			},
+		)
+	}
+
+	// Test 3: Cursor navigation (down/up, start/end)
+	{
+		ta := textarea.New()
+		ta.SetWidth(width)
+		ta.SetHeight(height)
+		ta.SetValue("A\nBB\nCCC")
+
+		ta.CursorDown()
+		afterDown := ta.Line()
+		ta.CursorEnd()
+		afterEnd := ta.Line()
+		ta.CursorStart()
+		afterStart := ta.Line()
+		ta.CursorUp()
+		afterUp := ta.Line()
+
+		ta.Blur()
+
+		fs.AddTestWithCategory("textarea_cursor_navigation", "unit",
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"value": "A\nBB\nCCC",
+			},
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"after_down":  afterDown,
+				"after_end":   afterEnd,
+				"after_start": afterStart,
+				"after_up":    afterUp,
+			},
+		)
+	}
+
+	// Test 4: Focus + blur toggles focused state
+	{
+		ta := textarea.New()
+		ta.SetWidth(width)
+		ta.SetHeight(height)
+
+		_, _ = ta.Update(textarea.Blink()) // Initialize cursor internals
+		_ = ta.Focus()
+		focused := ta.Focused()
+		ta.Blur()
+		blurred := ta.Focused()
+
+		fs.AddTestWithCategory("textarea_focus_blur", "unit",
+			map[string]interface{}{},
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"focused": focused,
+				"blurred": blurred,
+			},
+		)
+	}
+
+	// Test 5: Placeholder view renders placeholder when empty
+	{
+		ta := textarea.New()
+		ta.SetWidth(width)
+		ta.SetHeight(height)
+		ta.Placeholder = "Enter text..."
+		ta.Blur()
+
+		fs.AddTestWithCategory("textarea_placeholder_view", "unit",
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"placeholder": "Enter text...",
+				"width":       width,
+				"height":      height,
+			},
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"view": ta.View(),
+			},
+		)
+	}
+
+	// Test 6: Line numbers toggle affects view
+	{
+		ta := textarea.New()
+		ta.SetWidth(width)
+		ta.SetHeight(height)
+		ta.SetValue("one\ntwo")
+		ta.ShowLineNumbers = true
+		ta.Blur()
+
+		fs.AddTestWithCategory("textarea_line_numbers", "unit",
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"value":             "one\ntwo",
+				"show_line_numbers": true,
+				"width":             width,
+				"height":            height,
+			},
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"view": ta.View(),
+			},
+		)
+	}
+
+	// Test 7: Char limit is enforced for inserts
+	{
+		ta := textarea.New()
+		ta.SetWidth(width)
+		ta.SetHeight(height)
+		ta.CharLimit = 5
+		ta.InsertString("123456789")
+		ta.Blur()
+
+		fs.AddTestWithCategory("textarea_char_limit", "unit",
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"char_limit": 5,
+				"insert":     "123456789",
+			},
+			map[string]interface{}{
+				"value":  ta.Value(),
+				"length": ta.Length(),
+			},
+		)
+	}
+}
+
 func captureViewportTests(fs *capture.FixtureSet) {
 	// Test 1: Basic viewport creation
 	{
